summary: Migrated the fighter afterimage unit from a large raw asm file into a new C translation unit while preserving matching assembly for `ftCo_800C2600` and `ftCo_800C2FD8`. The PR added `src/melee/ft/ftafterimage.c`, updated prototypes and inferred stack/header data, exposed jump tables and labels needed by inline asm, expanded fighter and sword item type layouts around afterimage-related offsets, and added the new C object to `obj_files.mk`. Evidence indicates this was primarily a relocation/matching-enabling migration rather than a full high-level C rewrite: the new C file uses `ASM` functions with `nofralloc` under `MUST_MATCH` and `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` otherwise.
what_changed: Removed almost all text/sdata2 content from `asm/melee/ft/ftafterimage.s`, leaving the `ftCo_804D3C30` sdata string globalized.
Added `src/melee/ft/ftafterimage.c` with literals, jump-table definitions, and `ASM` implementations for `ftCo_800C2600(Fighter_GObj* gobj, u32 arg1)` and `ftCo_800C2FD8(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.
Moved constants such as `ftCo_804D8C88`, `ftCo_804D8C90`, `ftCo_804D8C98`, `ftCo_804D8CA0`, `ftCo_804D8CA4`, `ftCo_804D8CA8`, `ftCo_804D8CB0`, and `ftCo_804D8CB8` into the C file as typed literals.
Added generated jump tables `jtbl_803C6C00` and `jtbl_803C6C54` in C, with external label declarations in the header for their branch targets.
Replaced placeholder `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations in `ftafterimage.h` with typed prototypes and an inferred `_m2c_stack_ftCo_800C2600` stack layout.

Expanded `struct Fighter` around offsets `0x20B0` through `0x20FC`, replacing a filler block with individual `UNK_T` fields and floats `x20F8` and `x20FC`, while retaining `x2100` and the `x2101` union.
Expanded `struct itSword_UnkBytes` from only `x0` to byte fields `x0` through `x10`, matching byte accesses used by the afterimage code.
Added `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ftafterimage.c.o` to `obj_files.mk` while the asm object line still remained present for the leftover sdata symbol.
smart_moves: Kept the migration match-safe by embedding the original instructions inside `ASM` functions with `nofralloc`, rather than attempting a risky full high-level rewrite in one step.

Separated data ownership carefully: most literals and jump tables moved into C, but the small remaining `ftCo_804D3C30` sdata string stayed in the asm file and was made global so the C header could reference it.
Declared generated jump tables and branch labels explicitly, making switch/jump-table dependencies searchable and easier to revisit later.
Introduced an inferred stack struct for `ftCo_800C2600`, documenting large stack offsets and suspected array/padding regions even though the function body remained inline asm.
Updated dependent type layouts in both fighter and item headers so future high-level decompilation has named offsets for the afterimage trail positions, timing values, and sword color/byte data.
Included relevant GX, HSD, vector, item, and intrinsic headers in the new C file, establishing the dependency surface needed for future C conversion.
decomp_lessons: For large effects/rendering routines, an intermediate C file with inline asm can be valuable: it moves symbols, literals, jump tables, prototypes, and type information into the C ecosystem before the function is fully decompiled.
When a function uses branch tables, preserve label names and `jtbl_t` declarations explicitly; this keeps jump-table relocation and ordering visible during migration.
Do not hide struct knowledge in filler if the migrated code repeatedly accesses specific offsets. Even `UNK_T` and byte-field expansion can improve future decomp work by anchoring offsets.
Large stack frames can be documented with an `_m2c_stack_*` struct to capture inferred locals, padding, and uncertain array regions without overclaiming exact semantics.
If a leftover asm file only contains data needed by the migrated C unit, make the symbol global and declare it in the header rather than duplicating or guessing data placement.
A PR can be a useful decomp step even if the new C functions are still `ASM`; the matching report for this slice lists `ftCo_800C2600` and `ftCo_800C2FD8` at 0.00% diff.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_` prefix was retained for common fighter afterimage functions and literals, e.g. `ftCo_800C2600`, `ftCo_800C2FD8`, and `ftCo_804D8C88`.
Generated jump tables used `jtbl_` plus address naming, e.g. `jtbl_803C6C00` and `jtbl_803C6C54`.
Assembly labels migrated to C references used `lbl_` plus address naming, replacing local `.L_` labels from the asm excerpt.
Inferred stack-local names used `sp` plus hex offset inside `_m2c_stack_ftCo_800C2600`, with comments marking uncertain or inferred regions.
Unknown struct fields retained offset-based names such as `x20B0`, `x20F8`, `x2100`, and `itSword_UnkBytes::x10` rather than speculative semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `ASM void` functions with `nofralloc` to preserve the exact original instruction stream in the C translation unit.
Guarded function bodies with `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` producing `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`, and the inline asm body under `MUST_MATCH`.
Disabled formatting around inline asm with `clang-format off` comments to avoid perturbing assembly text.
Used explicit literal constants and typed float/double declarations to reproduce sdata2 values from the original asm.
Used `S32_TO_F32` and `U32_TO_F32` constants for integer-to-float conversion double literals.
Preserved jump-table ordering exactly in `jtbl_t` initializers for two switch-like tables.

Kept dependency includes for GX state setup calls such as `GXSetColorUpdate`, `GXSetAlphaUpdate`, `GXSetBlendMode`, `GXSetAlphaCompare`, `GXSetZMode`, `GXSetZCompLoc`, `GXSetNumTexGens`, `GXSetTevClampMode`, `GXSetNumTevStages`, `GXSetTevOrder`, `GXSetTevOp`, `GXSetNumChans`, `GXSetChanCtrl`, and `GXSetCullMode`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR merged very quickly on the same day it was created; no explicit reviewer rationale is available in the evidence.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert `ftCo_800C2600` or `ftCo_800C2FD8` from inline asm to high-level C.
Search for later renames or semantic typing of Fighter offsets `x20B0` through `x20FC` and flags at `x2100`/`x2101`.
Search for follow-up work on `itSword_UnkBytes` to determine the meaning of byte fields `x8` through `x10` used by afterimage rendering.
Check whether `ftCo_804D3C30` eventually moves out of `asm/melee/ft/ftafterimage.s` once the leftover asm data dependency is resolved.
Compare later jump-table handling for `jtbl_803C6C00` and `jtbl_803C6C54` to see if the switch structure was recovered.
